text: Israel set out with all he had and came to Beersheba, where he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
God spoke to Israel in a vision at night, calling his name; Jacob answered, here I am.
God said, I am God, the God of your father; do not be afraid to go to Egypt, for I will make you into a great nation there.
I will go down to Egypt with you, and I will surely bring you back again; Joseph's own hand will close your eyes.
Jacob left Beersheba, and his sons carried him, their children, and wives in the carts Pharaoh had sent.
They also took their livestock and possessions from Canaan, and Jacob and all his offspring went to Egypt.
He brought his sons, grandsons, daughters, and granddaughters, all his offspring, to Egypt with him.
These are the names of Israel's family who went to Egypt: Jacob and his sons, Reuben the firstborn.
Reuben's sons were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Karmi.
Simeon's sons were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jakin, Zohar, and Shaul, son of a Canaanite woman.
Levi's sons were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
Judah's sons were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah, though Er and Onan died in Canaan; Perez's sons were Hezron and Hamul.
Issachar's sons were Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron.
Zebulun's sons were Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
These were Leah's sons through Jacob, plus daughter Dinah; thirty-three persons in all.
Gad's sons were Zephon, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
Asher's sons were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and sister Serah; Beriah's sons were Heber and Malkiel.
These were the children of Zilpah, Laban's gift to Leah, sixteen persons in all.
The sons of Jacob's wife Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
In Egypt, Manasseh and Ephraim were born to Joseph by Asenath, daughter of Potiphera priest of On.
Benjamin's sons were Bela, Beker, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
These were Rachel's sons through Jacob, fourteen persons in all.
Dan's son was Hushim.
Naphtali's sons were Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
These were the sons of Bilhah, given to Rachel by Laban, seven persons in all.
All those who went to Egypt with Jacob, his direct descendants not counting his sons' wives, numbered sixty-six.
With Joseph's two sons born in Egypt, the members of Jacob's family who went to Egypt were seventy in all.
Jacob sent Judah ahead to Joseph to get directions to Goshen, and they arrived there.
Joseph had his chariot made ready and went to meet his father Israel in Goshen; as soon as he saw him, he embraced him and wept a long time.
Israel said to Joseph, now I am ready to die, since I have seen for myself that you are still alive.
Joseph told his brothers he would go tell Pharaoh his family had come from Canaan.
He would say the men are shepherds who tend livestock and have brought their flocks, herds, and everything they own.
When Pharaoh asks your occupation,
say you have tended livestock from boyhood, as did your fathers, so you may settle in Goshen, since shepherds are detestable to Egyptians.
